prompt: IT leadership wants each department charged for the laptops and support costs its users consume. Which financial model should HAM implement?
choices: Cost allocation or chargeback using cost centers, expense lines, and rate cards tied to assigned assets and support tasks
A flat global fee with no linkage between assets and departmental cost centers
Capitalize every device to a single enterprise cost center regardless of user department
Bill departments only when incidents are opened for device failures
explanation: Chargeback models use asset cost centers, operational expense lines, and task rate cards to allocate hardware and support spend to consuming departments.

prompt: Which of the following are considered operational expenses for hardware assets? (Choose three.)
choices: Maintenance agreement costs
Replacement parts
Resource costs for support
Delivery costs for initial purchase shipment
explanation: Operational expenses include ongoing maintenance, parts, and support labor. Initial delivery of purchased hardware is typically capital-related rather than operational expense in TCO context.

prompt: A hardware planner must forecast a three-year laptop budget for 8,000 users. Which analysis inputs should HAM provide?
choices: Refresh age targets, model TCO benchmarks, warranty coverage, and projected operational expense trends from asset analytics
Count of open incidents only without asset age or cost data
Discovery probe success rates as the sole budget driver
Number of catalog categories visible on the employee portal
explanation: Budget forecasting combines lifecycle age, TCO benchmarks, support costs, and refresh policies to project multi-year hardware spend.

prompt: Two laptop models have similar purchase price but one has higher repair costs and shorter warranty coverage. Which decision approach should finance use?
choices: Compare projected TCO including capital, maintenance, and support expenses using HAM analytics before standardizing the model
Select the lowest purchase price model without reviewing operational expense history
Mandate the oldest model in inventory to minimize procurement effort
Ignore warranty data because support costs are tracked outside ServiceNow
explanation: TCO analysis compares capital and operational costs—including maintenance and support—to inform refresh and standardization decisions.

prompt: Extended warranty quotes arrive for 500 servers nearing support expiration. How should HAM support the ROI decision?
choices: Compare warranty cost against historical maintenance expense and remaining useful life on the asset and model TCO reports
Approve every extended warranty automatically without reviewing asset age or repair history
Retire all servers immediately when the original warranty expires
Track warranty decisions only in email without updating contract fields on assets
explanation: Warranty ROI analysis weighs contract cost against maintenance history, asset age, and TCO projections available in HAM financial views.
